text:
# Optimizer scratchpad

## What MEMORY says was already tried
- cand_0001 (val 0.606): cancel_reservation guards (flown/cancelled), update_reservation_flights guards (flown, basic economy, origin/dest), update_reservation_baggages guard (removal), get_all_reservation_details loop tool, enriched get_reservation_details, policy rules 1-8.
- cand_0001 broke task 0 (regular member got unauthorized certificate).
- cand_0002 (val 0.660): cancel_reservation reason+eligibility, send_certificate eligibility+amount guard, book_reservation payment validation, modification_options, baggage nonfree>total guard, policy rules 9-11.
- cand_0003-0006 all REJECTED; key lessons: don't restrict get_all_reservation_details, don't add insurance-reason validation to cancel_reservation body (broke 8 tasks), don't add most_recent_reservation label to get_user_details (broke tasks), don't add origin/dest validation to book_reservation, don't add transfer_to_human rejection guard.

## Diagnosis + fan-out evidence
- Clusters ranked by (# failing tasks × trials), top-down:
  1. Insurance-based cancel without valid reason: tasks 43, 47 (20 cells) — agent sees "ELIGIBLE: has travel insurance" and cancels without verifying reason qualifies
  2. Transfer bail-out on doable requests: tasks 14, 24, 42 (30 cells) — agent transfers to human when it has the tools
  3. Duration miscalculation: tasks 44, 7 (20 cells) — agent mis-computes flight durations, picks wrong flights or states wrong totals
  4. Execution stall / write delay: tasks 15, 30, 32 (30 cells partial) — agent stalls in clarification loops and never executes write
  5. Wrong reservation identification: tasks 37, 38 (20 cells) — agent picks wrong "most recent" from list
  6. Missing COMMUNICATE value: task 7 (10 cells) — agent doesn't state exact refund total
  7. Passenger removal misconception: task 11 (7 cells) — agent cancels when user asks to remove passenger

- Two-phase evidence: Phase 1 used 9 parallel Explore subagents to diagnose tasks 7, 24, 42, 44, 1, 8, 10, 11, 14, 15, 30, 32, 33, 37, 38, 40, 43, 47. Phase 2 implemented serially in main agent (edits don't conflict since they target different sections of the same files).

## VERIFY-THE-FIX (one line per fix)
- trace task 43: agent sees cancellation_eligibility string → now contains "BUT only covers health/weather reasons. You MUST verify the user's reason qualifies" → agent prompted to check reason before cancelling
- trace task 47: same fix — eligibility message now warns insurance only covers health/weather, preventing auto-cancel for birthday/convenience reasons
- trace task 44: agent calls search_direct_flight → now returns duration_minutes=<computed> → agent can correctly identify >240min (>4h) segments without manual math errors
- trace task 7: agent calls cancel_reservation → return now includes total_refund_amount=<value> + refund_note="ALWAYS state this exact amount" → agent prompted to communicate the figure
- trace task 14: transfer_to_human_agents docstring now explicitly says "payment balances available via get_user_details" → agent knows it CAN look up balances
- trace task 37/38: get_all_reservation_details now returns sorted by created_at descending → most recent reservation is first in list, reducing misidentification
- trace task 15/30: policy rule 1 strengthened — "never let more than one response pass between confirmation and execution" → agent executes write faster
- trace task 24/42: transfer_to_human_agents docstring now says "one sub-request failed but others still actionable — handle each independently" + policy rule 13 → agent continues with actionable parts
- trace task 11: update_reservation_passengers docstring now says "CANNOT add or remove passengers" → agent knows not to cancel as workaround

## Handover for next iteration

- Approaches tried this iteration (1 concrete line each):
  - Enriched get_reservation_details: insurance eligibility warning (health/weather only), total_amount_paid field
  - Enriched cancel_reservation return: total_refund_amount + refund_note (dict return)
  - Enriched update_reservation_flights return: price_difference field (dict return)
  - Added duration_minutes computation to search_direct_flight (in-code enrichment)
  - Sorted get_all_reservation_details by created_at descending
  - Expanded transfer_to_human_agents docstring with more "Do NOT transfer" cases
  - Strengthened update_reservation_passengers docstring (cannot add/remove passengers)
  - Policy rule 1 strengthened (immediate execution after confirm, no re-confirmation)
  - Policy rule 4 rewritten (use pre-computed duration_minutes)
  - Policy rule 8 strengthened (insurance requires health/weather reason verification)
  - Added policy rules 12-16 (insurance reason, independent sub-requests, payment info, duration_minutes, pick-the-cheapest)

- Lessons learned (general):
  - cand_0004 proved: NEVER add insurance-reason validation to cancel_reservation BODY (breaks passing tasks that correctly pass health/weather). Fix via MESSAGING only (eligibility string warning).
  - cand_0003/0004 proved: NEVER add most_recent_reservation label to get_user_details (breaks tasks). Just SORT the list.
  - Returning dict instead of Reservation from write tools is safe if type annotation matches — the framework serializes to JSON either way.
  - cand_0006's duration_minutes enrichment was rejected due to OTHER changes (duration policy, expanded transfer guard, new rules) — the enrichment itself is likely safe in isolation.

- Recommendation / what to focus on next:
  - Task 7 (always-failing): The user-simulator ###STOP### early in most trials — this is partly uncontrollable. The COMMUNICATE check (value 1628) may now pass with the enriched return if the conversation progresses far enough.
  - Task 24 (always-failing): Complex multi-part request with cheapest-flight search. Hard to fix without overfitting. May improve with rule 13 (independent sub-requests).
  - Task 1 (reward 0.20): Gold expects NO cancellation despite 24h eligibility. Unclear why — may be a case where the gold expects the agent to get the reason first and then discover it doesn't qualify. Investigate the passing trials.
  - Tasks 37/38: If sorting alone doesn't help, consider enriching each reservation's description with an index label ("Reservation 1 of 5, most recently booked").

- Rules still living as prose that SHOULD become in-code checks next iteration:
  - Insurance reason validation: currently in eligibility MESSAGE only (cannot be in cancel body per cand_0004 lesson). Could add a SOFT warning to cancel_reservation return when has_insurance was the sole eligibility basis.
  - "Cannot add insurance after booking": no guard in update tools yet
  - Transfer-to-human rejection: currently docstring-only (cand_0004's keyword guard broke tasks)

- Approaches that regressed AS IMPLEMENTED (a better-designed version may still work):
  - Insurance-reason validation in cancel_reservation body (cand_0004) — broke 8 passing tasks. Redesigned as eligibility MESSAGE warning instead.
  - most_recent_reservation label (cand_0003/0004) — broke tasks by biasing agent toward most recent. Redesigned as sort-only (no label).
  - duration_minutes enrichment (cand_0006) — rejected alongside other changes. Re-attempted in isolation here.
